events.title: Gateway of India & Taj Palace Walk
events.description: Start your trip with Mumbai's most iconic landmark. Walk along the waterfront and admire the colonial architecture.

events.title: Breakfast at Leopold Cafe
events.description: Historic cafe since 1871. Try the Eggs Benedict and fresh juices.

events.title: Colaba Causeway Shopping
events.description: Browse antiques, jewellery, and local crafts along this vibrant street.

events.title: Chai Break at Irani Cafe
events.description: Rest your feet with a refreshing cutting chai and bun maska.

events.title: Marine Drive Sunset Walk
events.description: Stroll along the Queen's Necklace as the sun sets over the Arabian Sea.

events.title: Dinner at Bademiya
events.description: Mumbai's legendary late-night kebab spot. Must try the Seekh Kebab rolls.

events.title: Morning Jog at Jogger's Park
events.description: Sea-facing jogging track in Bandra West. Fresh air and stunning views.

events.title: Bandra Street Art Walk
events.description: Explore colourful murals and graffiti in Bandra's lanes.

events.title: Lunch at Bastian
events.description: Modern seafood restaurant with stunning interiors.

events.title: Sanjay Gandhi National Park & Kanheri Caves
events.description: Ancient Buddhist caves inside a lush national park.

events.title: Sunset Chai
events.description: Relax with a cup of chai and reflect on the day.

events.title: Prithvi Theatre Show
events.description: Catch a play at Mumbai's beloved intimate theatre.

events.title: Dharavi Creative Tour
events.description: Guided tour of Asia's largest creative economy. See pottery, recycling, and leather workshops.

events.title: Street Food Trail
events.description: Vada pav, pani puri, and pav bhaji at Girgaon Chowpatty.

events.title: Chor Bazaar Treasure Hunt
events.description: Hunt for antiques and vintage finds in Mumbai's famous flea market.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Rest at a nearby cafe before dinner.

events.title: Farewell Dinner at The Table
events.description: Farm-to-table dining with panoramic Mumbai views.

culturalNudges: Remove shoes before entering temples
Dress modestly at religious sites
Haggling is expected at street markets
Tipping 10% at restaurants is customary
Avoid tap water — drink bottled water

events.title: Marina Bay Sands & SkyPark
events.description: Start with the iconic infinity pool views from the SkyPark observation deck.

events.title: Gardens by the Bay
events.description: Walk through Supertree Grove and Cloud Forest. Don't miss the waterfall.

events.title: Lunch at Hawker Chan
events.description: World's cheapest Michelin-starred meal. Try the Soya Sauce Chicken.

events.title: Chinatown Heritage Walk
events.description: Explore temples, shophouses, and traditional medicine halls.

events.title: Tea Break
events.description: Cool down with a teh tarik at a nearby kopitiam.

events.title: Dinner at Newton Food Centre
events.description: Hawker centre feast — satay, chilli crab, carrot cake.

events.title: Sentosa Island
events.description: Beach, Universal Studios, or S.E.A. Aquarium. Take the cable car.

events.title: Lunch at Vivo City
events.description: Waterfront mall with diverse food options.

events.title: Singapore Botanic Gardens
events.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Visit the National Orchid Garden.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Rest at a garden cafe.

events.title: Cocktails at Long Bar, Raffles
events.description: Home of the Singapore Sling. Colonial elegance.

events.title: Little India Walking Tour
events.description: Vibrant colours, spice shops, and Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple.

events.title: Brunch at Tekka Centre
events.description: Authentic Indian hawker food — roti prata, biryani, masala dosa.

events.title: ArtScience Museum
events.description: Interactive digital art exhibition by teamLab.

events.title: Coffee Break
events.description: Specialty coffee at a local roaster.

events.title: Clarke Quay Evening
events.description: Riverside dining and nightlife. Great sunset views.

documentChecklist: SG Arrival Card (submit online before departure)
culturalNudges: Chewing gum is banned
No eating/drinking on MRT trains (fine up to SGD 500)
Tipping is not expected
Jaywalking is fined
Carry an umbrella — tropical showers are frequent

events.title: British Museum
events.description: One of the world's greatest museums. Free entry. Start with the Rosetta Stone.

events.title: Lunch at Borough Market
events.description: London's oldest food market. Try the grilled cheese and fresh oysters.

events.title: Tate Modern
events.description: Free modern art gallery in a converted power station. Views from Level 10.

events.title: Tea Time
events.description: Traditional afternoon tea at a nearby cafe.

events.title: Dinner at Dishoom
events.description: Bombay-inspired cafe. The bacon naan is legendary.

events.title: Hyde Park Morning Run
events.description: Jog around the Serpentine. Fresh air in the heart of London.

events.title: Tower of London
events.description: Crown Jewels, Beefeaters, and 1000 years of history.

events.title: Fish & Chips at Poppies
events.description: Award-winning fish and chips in Spitalfields.

events.title: Sky Garden
events.description: Free rooftop garden with panoramic London views.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Rest at a local pub.

events.title: Dinner at The Ivy
events.description: Iconic West End restaurant. Smart casual dress code.

events.title: Camden Market
events.description: Eclectic market with street food, vintage fashion, and live music.

events.title: Lunch at Camden Market
events.description: Global street food — try the Thai, Ethiopian, or Venezuelan stalls.

events.title: Regent's Park & Primrose Hill
events.description: Beautiful gardens and the best skyline view in London.

events.title: Pub Break
events.description: Classic British pub experience.

events.title: West End Show
events.description: Catch a musical or play in London's Theatre District.

documentChecklist: Oyster card or contactless payment for transport
culturalNudges: Stand on the right on escalators
Queue etiquette is taken seriously
Tipping 10-15% at restaurants
Pubs often stop serving food by 21:00
Weather is unpredictable — always carry a layer

events.title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
events.description: World-class art spanning 5000 years. Start with Egyptian Temple of Dendur.

events.title: Lunch at Joe's Pizza
events.description: Iconic NYC pizza since 1975. Get a classic cheese slice.

events.title: High Line Walk
events.description: Elevated park on retired railway tracks with art installations.

events.title: Coffee Break
events.description: Specialty coffee at a Chelsea cafe.

events.title: Times Square at Night
events.description: Experience the neon-lit energy of the Crossroads of the World.

events.title: Dinner in Hell's Kitchen
events.description: Diverse restaurant row with every cuisine imaginable.

events.title: Central Park Morning Run
events.description: Jog the reservoir loop with Manhattan skyline views.

events.title: Brooklyn Bridge Walk
events.description: Walk from Brooklyn to Manhattan. Stop for photos at DUMBO.

events.title: DUMBO Food Scene
events.description: Grimaldi's pizza or Time Out Market Brooklyn.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Rest at Brooklyn Bridge Park with Manhattan views.

events.title: SoHo Shopping
events.description: Cast-iron architecture and designer boutiques.

events.title: Dinner at Le Bernardin
events.description: World-class seafood. 3 Michelin stars. Jacket required.

events.title: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island
events.description: Book ferry tickets in advance. Allow the full morning.

events.title: Lunch at Sweetgreen
events.description: Healthy fast-casual. Great for a quick nutritious meal.

events.title: 9/11 Memorial & Museum
events.description: Powerful and moving. Allow 2 hours minimum.

events.title: Rooftop Break
events.description: Enjoy views from a Financial District rooftop bar.

events.title: Broadway Show
events.description: Catch a hit musical on Broadway.

culturalNudges: Tipping 18-20% is expected at restaurants
Subway runs 24/7 but can be unreliable late night
Jaywalking is common but technically illegal
Sales tax (8.875%) is added at checkout, not shown on price tags
Carry ID — some bars card everyone

events.title: Senso-ji Temple
events.description: Tokyo's oldest temple. Walk through Kaminarimon Gate and Nakamise-dori.

events.title: Sushi Breakfast at Tsukiji
events.description: Fresh sushi and seafood at the outer market.

events.title: Meiji Jingu Shrine
events.description: Serene forested shrine in the heart of Shibuya. Bow at the torii.

events.title: Matcha Break
events.description: Traditional matcha and wagashi at a tea house.

events.title: Harajuku & Takeshita Street
events.description: Youth fashion, crepe shops, and kawaii culture.

events.title: Ramen at Ichiran
events.description: Solo booth ramen — fill out the preference sheet for your perfect bowl.

events.title: Shibuya Crossing & Hachiko
events.description: World's busiest intersection. Photo from Starbucks above.

events.title: Tonkatsu at Maisen
events.description: Famous tonkatsu in a converted bathhouse. Heritage vibes.

events.title: Shimokitazawa
events.description: Bohemian neighbourhood — vintage shops, vinyl records, indie cafes.

events.title: Coffee at Bear Pond
events.description: Legendary third-wave coffee shop.

events.title: Roppongi Hills & Mori Art Museum
events.description: Contemporary art with panoramic Tokyo views from the Sky Deck.

events.title: Izakaya in Shinjuku
events.description: Japanese pub culture. Order yakitori, edamame, and sake.

events.title: Morning at Yoyogi Park
events.description: Peaceful morning walk or jog through Tokyo's green lung.

events.title: Akihabara Electric Town
events.description: Anime, manga, electronics, and gaming culture.

events.title: Conveyor Belt Sushi
events.description: Fun and affordable kaitenzushi experience.

events.title: teamLab Borderless
events.description: Immersive digital art experience. Book in advance.

events.title: Breathing Room
events.description: Rest with a cold brew.

events.title: Farewell Dinner at Gonpachi
events.description: The restaurant that inspired Kill Bill. Yakitori and soba.

documentChecklist: Japan visa (if applicable — many countries have visa exemption)
Japan Rail Pass (if travelling between cities)
Pocket WiFi or eSIM (essential for navigation)
culturalNudges: Bow when greeting — depth indicates formality
Remove shoes when entering homes or traditional restaurants
Don't eat while walking
Silence phones on trains
Tipping is not practiced and can be seen as rude
Separate your rubbish — recycling is strict
